title: Async Context, Abort Signals, Deadlines, and Cleanup · Transfer Build
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers async context, abort signals, deadlines, and cleanup into a new request context, budget, cancellation, and cleanup ledger. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of request context, budget, cancellation, and cleanup ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Use AsyncLocalStorage run, enterWith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization.
Compose client disconnect, service deadline, dependency timeout, and shutdown with AbortSignal.timeout and AbortSignal.any while preserving the first cause.
Allocate request time among queueing, dependencies, response, and cleanup with monotonic evidence and no negative or expanding child budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Async Local Storage Scope
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 8414 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reserve cleanup time and combine client abort with one dependency budget; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Async Local Storage Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use asynclocalstorage run, enterwith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Async Local Storage Scope, keep Async Local Storage Scope connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reserve cleanup time and combine client abort with one dependency budget; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Own background promise lifetimes, observe rejection and multiple-resolution failures, fail safely, and distinguish crash recovery from request error handling.
